questionHtml: Suggest why hydrogen chloride, HCl, has a lower boiling point than hydrogen cyanide, HCN.
markschemeHtml:
HCN has stronger dipole–dipole attraction ✔

 

Do not accept reference to H-bonds.

meta.title: Suggest why hydrogen chloride, HCl, has a lower boiling point than hydrogen cyanide, HCN.
breadcrumbLabels: Structure 2. Models of bonding and structure

Structure 2.2.9—Given comparable molar mass, the relative strengths of intermolecular forces are generally: London (dispersion) forces < dipole–dipole forces < hydrogen bonding. Explain the physical properties of covalent substances to include volatility, electrical conductivity and solubility in terms of their structure.
